summary: Snell's law describes how light bends when it crosses between media: n₁·sin θ₁ = n₂·sin θ₂. Angles are measured from the normal, not the surface.
method: Identify the two media (their refractive indices n₁ and n₂) and which angle is known.
Measure all angles from the normal to the surface, in degrees.
Decide which quantity is unknown — an index or an angle.
Rearrange n₁·sin θ₁ = n₂·sin θ₂ for the unknown.
If solving for an angle gives sin θ > 1, the ray is totally internally reflected — no refracted angle exists.

example.title: Light entering water from air
steps: Solve for θ₂: sin θ₂ = n₁·sin θ₁ / n₂.
Take the inverse sine: θ₂ = arcsin(0.376).
